methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Terranova is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Laurence Allen that has raised $7M to date. The comparable universe in Robotics & Manufacturing includes 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private peers, notably 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics. However, peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within narrow filters—only 12 same-sector peers exist, with none meeting the minimum threshold of 3 datapoints in tightly-defined cohorts.

Accordingly, stage-progression methodology anchors the analysis. Using total capital raised ($7M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $17.5M. This single applicable method constrains the triangulated range to $15M (low), $17.5M (median), and $20.1M (high), reflecting a ±15% sensitivity band around the point estimate.

The analysis supports a valuation in the $15M to $20M range, with $17.5M as the central point estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of granular comparable transaction data at seed stage within the robotics subsector and dependency on stage-progression assumptions that may not reflect Terranova's specific risk profile, technology maturity, or market traction relative to sector peers.
